aminosanStory.title: Where it all began.
aminosanStory.body1: In 1988, the first bottle of Aminosan was born.
aminosanStory.body2: Created to deliver plant nutrition at a time when the idea was still taking its first steps in Brazil.
aminosanStory.oldBottleAlt: The first bottle of Aminosan, a historic piece displayed as a relic
aminosanStory.newBottleAlt: Current Aminosan bottle, the product in use across the Juma Agro line
aminosanStory.videoAlt: Transition from the old Aminosan bottle to the current bottle
aminosanStory.callout1: More than 40 years later, the same amino acid.
aminosanStory.callout2: Today, across eight crops: soybeans, corn, coffee, cotton, beans, citrus, tomato and potato.
aminosanStory.callout3Label: In soybeans, 10 to 14 extra bags per hectare in trials.
aminosanStory.callout3Source: Source: DETEC (Taquarivaí, SP) and Terras Gerais (Lavras, MG) field trials.
aminosanStory.callout4: And today, a full line: thirteen products, from planting to harvest.

ourStory.body: More than 45 years ago, Mr. Julio Matino took the first step, driven by courage, hard work and love for the land. By his side, his children carry the legacy forward with excellence, management and vision, leading Juma Agro to new horizons.
ourStory.cta: A legacy that builds the future.
ourStory.familyAlt: Fábio, Julio and Julio Matino, founder and directors of Juma Agro, standing with arms crossed

cana.label: more stalks in sugarcane
cana.source: Acorda Cana · source: Juma Agro field trials

problem.kicker: The gap that defines the harvest
problem.titleSentence: The toll that usually goes unnoticed sits between what the field delivers and what it could deliver.
problem.bodyRest: An uneven stand at planting, a dry spell that stalls the plant in January, a bloom that aborts in the heat, a spray the wind carries away: each of these episodes costs bags that nobody enters in a spreadsheet, because they do not show up as cost. They show up as a smaller harvest.
problem.paragraph2: The difference between a good field and a record field is almost never about spending more. It is about getting through every phase without leaving potential behind.

solution.title: The phase by phase program
solution.intro: For each crop, Juma defines the sequence: which product goes in, at which phase, for which purpose. The program starts at seed treatment and runs through grain fill, using the spray calendar you already follow. Every combination has been tested in field trials against a control, and the source of the result is published right next to the number.

step1.title: Choose your crop.
step1.body: Soybeans, corn, coffee, sugarcane and six others.

culturesGrid.title: Your crop has a program
culturesGrid.intro: Every field decides its season in different phases. Choose yours and see the challenges of each phase and what to apply in each one.

numbers.title: What the trials measured
numbers.soja: Soybeans: +5.8 bags/ha with Acorda Ultra and +10 to +14 bags/ha with Aminosan, in DETEC (Taquarivaí, SP) and Terras Gerais (Lavras, MG) field trials.
numbers.milho: Corn: +13.4 bags/ha with Acorda Ultra and +9.33 bags/ha in leafhopper management with KMEP Ultra (Rehagro).
numbers.cana: Sugarcane: +6.87 t/ha of stalks with Acorda Cana, in field trials.
numbers.cafe: Coffee: up to 74% control of leaf miner with KMEP Ultra (Rehagro).
numbers.disclaimer: Trial results compared with an untreated control. In the field, the number varies with climate, soil and management. That is why we publish the source of each one.

productsPage.intro: Thirteen products designed to support the grower from seed treatment to complete management — formulated over 40 years of Brazilian field experience.

acordaCana.description: Rooting, vigor and higher productivity in sugarcane, from the very first day.

acordaUltra.description: Strong establishment and deep rooting from the very first day in the soil.

aduban.description: Balanced soil and better nutrient uptake for the young plant.

aminosan.description: 40+ years as Brazil's reference amino acid — vigor, roots and stress tolerance.

fitofert.description: Better fruit set, grain fill and productivity in the reproductive phase.

linhaRevigo.description: Complete foliar line to correct deficiencies across every crop.

revigophosAmino.description: Fast energy for the plant and accelerated recovery after stress events.

revigoCobreUltra.description: Complexed copper: physiological strength and plant resistance.

kmepUltra.description: Boosts insecticides and improves efficiency in pest control.

linhaRedutan.description: Spray quality, less drift and greater operational efficiency.

supermix.description: Adjuvant for uniform spray, no clogging and greater compatibility.

revigoMilho.description: Strategic nutrition for second-crop corn — real gain per hectare.

revigoPasto.description: Pasture recovery, weight gain and higher stocking rate per hectare.

aboutPage.titleStart: Over 40 years in the field, alongside those who
aboutPage.intro: Juma was born from the restlessness of a man of the land. Founded by Mr. Julio Matino, the company grew by turning agronomic knowledge into products that raise the productivity of the Brazilian grower — without ever losing touch with the field.
aboutPage.historyTitle: Four decades counted in the field.
aboutPage.historyIntro: Milestones that show how Juma grew side by side with the Brazilian grower.

y1985.title: The beginning, in inland São Paulo
y1985.desc: Mr. Julio Matino founds Juma in Mogi Mirim with the goal of offering Brazilian growers the most advanced plant nutrition available — starting with locally formulated foliar fertilizers.

y1992.title: Launch of Aminosan®
y1992.desc: The first plant-origin amino acid from Juma reaches the market and would go on, in the following decades, to become the absolute reference in nutritional management of coffee, soybeans and corn in Brazil.

y2003.title: Expansion to other crops
y2003.desc: Juma broadens its portfolio to sugarcane, cotton and perennial crops. The Revigo line is born — crop-specific foliar products to correct deficiencies in every cultivar.

y2015.title: Application technology
y2015.desc: With the arrival of the Redutan and Supermix lines, Juma begins offering spray technology — application quality, less drift and greater operational efficiency.

y2024.title: 13 products · 10 crops
y2024.desc: Juma's current portfolio — products integrated into complete nutritional programs, supporting professional management from planting to harvest across Brazil.

aboutPage.valuesIntro: Principles that have held Juma steady for four decades — and continue to guide us.

v1.title: Alongside the grower
v1.desc: Agronomic team in the field, across every region. Decisions made where results happen.

v2.title: Applied knowledge
v2.desc: Continuous research and real trials with partner growers — science translated into results.

v3.title: Responsibility
v3.desc: Productivity through responsible management, efficient formulations and respect for soil and water.

aboutPage.purposeBody: More than a slogan. It is what sustains every decision we make — from the lab to the first sprayer leaving a farm at the break of dawn.
aboutPage.galleryIntro: Headquarters in Mogi Mirim, SP — technical team operating across more than 10 Brazilian states.
aboutPage.ctaBody: 40 years of experience ready to work for your next season.
